images.title: Derived Play: Fold Rise Rally
images.caption: 从层压提示台派生出的圆形冷却架接力玩法：玩家抽 Fold Tile、叠 Context Layer、花 Butter Token、放 Steam Edit、关 Crumb Mask、对 Pan Hoop、贴 Caption Score、选 Glaze Chip，只有所有琥珀月牙窗仍然匹配时才能敲响 Rise Check。
images.alt: Square overhead product photograph of a round cooling-rack tabletop rally with crescent layer tiles, context layers, butter tokens, steam edit tabs, crumb mask gates, pan hoops, caption score strips, glaze chips, and rise check bell
images.prompt:
Create one polished public-gallery image titled "Derived Play: Fold Rise Rally".

This is a derived playable mode from Prompt Lamination Deck, but it must look like a different rules object: a cooperative tabletop fold-and-check rally using soft pastry-layer tiles and a circular cooling rack score path, not the same cutaway lamination deck, not a recipe, not a tasting menu, not a grid, not cards, not a contact sheet, not a rectangular board, not a proof press, not a loom, not a fossil drawer, not a wardrobe carousel, not a water basin, not a snowglobe rack, not a toy latch.

Output format: one 1:1 square overhead product photograph of a round wire cooling-rack arena on a neutral table. Inside are twelve soft crescent layer tiles, three pan-ratio hoops, crumb mask gates, steam edit tabs, tiny caption scoring strips, glaze chips, butter-reference tokens, and a small rise-check bell. The only motif is the invented amber crescent-window repeated on safe felt tokens. No people, no hands, no logos, no real bakery, no real recipe.

Playable rules shown through objects: players draft one Fold Tile, tuck it under one Context Layer, spend a Butter Token, place one Steam Edit tab, close one Crumb Mask gate, align one Pan Hoop, attach one Caption Score strip, choose one Glaze Chip, then ring the Rise Check bell only if all visible crescent-window motifs still match.

Readable short labels: Fold Tile, Context Layer, Butter Token, Steam Edit, Crumb Mask, Pan Hoop, Caption Score, Glaze Chip, Rise Check. Visual language: premium tactile tabletop prototype, round stainless cooling rack, ivory felt pastry layers, amber windows, teal tokens, graphite labels, warm studio shadows, playful but sophisticated. Everything is fictional, original, unbranded, rights-safe, non-political, harmless, non-adult, and non-graphic. Avoid public figures, real brands, celebrity likenesses, copyrighted characters, explicit material, harmful instructions, weapons, gore, and living-artist style imitation.

images.title: Derived Play: Settling Globe Relay
images.caption: 从雪景球沉降室派生的五球接力玩法：玩家沿弧形架传递小球，花 Shake Token，调 Edit Flake、Mask Sleeve、Style Bead、Caption Tag 和 Crop Ring，等 Settling Timer 停下后用 Proof Loupe 检查同一座三拱月港是否仍然可识别。
images.alt: Wide low-oblique product photograph of crescent rack with five small transparent snowglobes, ceramic moon-harbors, shake tokens, edit flakes, mask sleeves, style beads, caption tags, crop rings, settling timer, proof loupe, and relay score
images.prompt:
Create one polished public-gallery image titled "Derived Play: Settling Globe Relay".

This is a derived playable mode from Prompt Settling Chamber, but it must look like a different rules object: a cooperative shake-and-observe tabletop relay using five small transparent snowglobes in a curved rack. It is not the same single cutaway globe, not a grid, not a contact sheet, not cards, not a rectangular board game, not a cabinet, not a water basin, not a wardrobe, not a fossil drawer, not a toy latch, not a greenhouse, not a weather station, not a planetarium, not a music box, not a flipbook, not a loom, not a proof press, not an optical bench, not a magnetic field device.

Output format: one wide 16:9 low-oblique product photograph of a crescent-shaped tabletop rack holding five palm-size clear globes. Each globe contains a tiny version of the same invented ceramic moon-harbor with three teal archways and one amber beacon, plus different harmless flakes and transparent rings. Around the rack are a sand timer, small shake tokens, caption clips, mask sleeves, style beads, and a final proof loupe. No people, no hands, no real places, no logos.

Playable rules shown through objects: players pass a globe along the crescent rack, spend one Shake Token, add or remove one Edit Flake, slide one Mask Sleeve, choose one Style Bead, clip one Caption Tag, align one Crop Ring, then wait for the Settling Timer. A point scores only if the proof loupe still sees the same three teal arches and amber beacon after the flakes settle.

images.title: Derived Play: Buoy Chain Regatta
images.caption: 从潮池浮标链派生出的圆形水盆接力玩法：玩家拨 Current Ring、移动浮标但不能断链，并依次放 Anchor Token、转 Drift Ghost、换 Tide Edit、停 Reef Blocker、夹 Caption Pennant、花 Lantern Charge，最后用 Proof Cove 和 Chain Score 检查同一枚虚构缺口螺旋贝是否保持可识别。
images.alt: Square overhead product photograph of a round ceramic water basin game with floating modular buoys, current rings, drift ghosts, tide edit buoy, reef blockers, caption pennants, lantern charges, proof coves, chain score markers, and teal notched spiral shell tokens
images.prompt:
Create one polished public-gallery image titled "Derived Play: Buoy Chain Regatta".

This is a derived playable mode from Prompt Buoy Chain, but it must look like a different rules object: a cooperative floating-token regatta played in a circular tabletop water arena, not the same panoramic calibration tray, not a navigation chart, not a boat race, not real marine equipment, not a 2x3 grid, not cards, not a contact sheet, not a rectangular board game, not a poster, not a toy latch, not a fossil drawer, not a wardrobe carousel, not a library cabinet, not a terrain model, not a weather station, not a kite tunnel, not a patchbay.

Output format: one square 1:1 overhead product photograph of a round shallow ceramic basin filled with clear water. Inside the basin are twelve floating modular buoys, three soft reef blockers, tiny washable caption pennants, translucent current rings, a turn dial clipped to the rim, and four proof coves around the edge. The only subject motif is an invented teal spiral shell with two square notches, repeated on safe foam tokens. No people, no hands, no real places, no boats, no animals.

Playable rules shown through objects: players take turns flicking a small current ring, then must move one buoy without breaking the cotton-cord chain. Each round they place an Anchor Token, rotate a Current Ring, slide a Drift Ghost, swap one Tide Edit buoy, park one Reef Blocker, clip one Caption Pennant, spend a Lantern Charge, and check that the teal notched spiral shell reaches the correct Proof Cove. A score only counts when the subject is still recognizable, the caption pennant is readable, the reef blocker excludes one unwanted motif, and the final proof cove repeats the same shell.

images.prompt:

Purpose: derive a playable image-prompt game from the sand-table use-case idea, but make it a distinct rules object: a competitive kinetic-sand terrain arena with molded pieces, survey gates, scoring beads, change-boundary fences, light pegs, and crop frames. images.prompt:

images.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: GPT Image 2 gallery derived play modes board, 2K landscape contact sheet
Primary request: Create a second polished 3x2 contact sheet of six NEW game-like play mechanics derived from the companion Images 2.0 use-case board. These must be playable frameworks for experimenting with GPT Image 2 outputs, not repeats of earlier recorded mechanics such as product-to-flavor-lab, app-to-board-game, poster-to-mini-comic, Claim Court, Access Route Remix, Director's Cut Ladder, Aging Oracle, Taste Telemetry Duel, Edge-Case Hunt, Manual Maze, Price Canon Kitchen, Prop Continuity Poker, Signage Babel Sprint, Figure Ladder Duel, Locked-Room Remix, Rubric Arena, Lighting DJ, Fix-It Quest, prompt relay, constraint bingo, regression detective, variant draft arena, scene grammar kit, scale-shift microscope, wardrobe audit, gallery speedrun, drill-path puzzle, material truth table, waveform jam, or maintenance triage.
Panels:
1. Source Stamp Sprint: players draft a current-data visual card, then earn points for visible date, source, uncertainty, and non-misleading summary cues.
2. Alpha Edge Trial: players generate cutout sticker sheets, then inspect corners, holes, shadows, and hairline edges with loupe tokens and pass/fail mats.
3. Ratio Relay Run: teams adapt one image brief across 3:1, 1:1, 9:16, and poster formats; penalties for changed hero object, lost message, or cramped text.
4. Safety Sketch Court: players convert rough product sketches into safe mockups, defending each change with hazard cards and material tokens.
5. Tiny-Type Tavern: teams build dense multilingual menu boards; judges order items using only the generated signs, scoring legibility and hierarchy.
6. Deck Pick Poker: each player generates a style deck from one brief, then bids on which direction best balances clarity, cost, novelty, and reuse.
Style/medium: premium tabletop game documentation spread, realistic cards, score sheets, timer tiles, loupes, tokens, mini output images, pencils, no real brands.
Composition/framing: 3 columns by 2 rows, six clearly separated panels; each panel shows title, components, round structure, scoring evidence, and a small generated-output sample.
Lighting/mood: warm studio tabletop lighting, collaborative design lab energy, precise enough for documentation.

images.prompt:
Create a single high-resolution 3-panel derived-play concept board. Three vertical panels side by side, separated by thin grid lines. These are playful derivations from the previous three concepts, not the same images.

Panel 1: Poster-to-AR installation. The "月光档案馆 / MOONLIGHT ARCHIVE" event poster has become an immersive street AR wayfinding scene. A person holds a phone in front of the poster, and the phone screen shows glowing moonlit archive shelves extending into the real street with elegant arrows and tiny floating labels. Keep the bilingual event identity visible but not dominant. Cinematic night photography, plausible UI overlay, legible key words only.

Panel 2: Cutaway-to-learning-kit. The ramen vending machine cutaway has been transformed into a tactile STEM learning kit on a classroom table: magnetic machine layers, labeled cards, tiny pump/valve tokens, a fold-out instruction mat, and kids' hands assembling it. Labels on cards in Chinese: "汤泵", "面仓", "蒸汽阀", "调味轮". Warm documentary product photography, organized parts, educational but fun.

Panel 3: Character-to-merch system. The Lumo moon courier mascot becomes a cohesive merchandise launch board: sticker sheet, enamel pin, tote bag, small plush, postcard, and shipping-box tape. Maintain the same moon-shaped courier with blue scarf and satchel. Include small clean brand text "Lumo Courier" on packaging only. Bright studio flat lay, consistent brand colors, realistic materials.

Overall: polished concept research board, high detail, no watermark, no clutter, keep all text minimal and as specified, avoid copyrighted styles or characters.
